# Mastering Node.js, Second Edition (Sandro Pasquali)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Understanding the Node Environment](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/01-understanding-the-node-environment.md): Node.js systems programming, V8 JavaScript engine optimization, high-concurrency network application architecture, non-blocking I/O handling, and CommonJS module composition.
    - [Understanding Asynchronous Event-Driven Programming](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/02-understanding-asynchronous-event-driven-programming.md): Writing, refactoring, or analyzing asynchronous Node.js code, including managing concurrency, configuring the event loop, handling I/O operations, writing network or filesystem event listeners, managing timers, handling Node.js errors and exceptions, and implementing flow control utilizing callbacks, Promises, async/await, or Generators.
    - [Streaming Data Across Nodes and Clients](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/03-streaming-data-across-nodes-and-clients.md): Node.js I/O operations, reading or writing files, building network servers or clients (HTTP/HTTPS/TCP/UDP), proxying network traffic, processing large datasets or payloads, manipulating memory buffers, or explicitly working with the Node.js `stream`, `http`, `https`, `url`, or `querystring` modules.
    - [Using Node to Access the Filesystem](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/04-using-node-to-access-the-filesystem.md): Node.js filesystem operations (I/O), path manipulations, static file serving, HTTP caching implementations, multipart file uploads, and building desktop applications bridging Node.js and Chromium via Electron.
    - [Managing Many Simultaneous Client Connections](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/05-managing-many-simultaneous-client-connections.md): Designing, refactoring, or managing high-concurrency Node.js network applications, configuring Express routing, implementing session and state management (especially with Redis), designing long-polling communication, or implementing authentication mechanisms including Basic Authentication, Challenge-Response Handshakes, and JSON Web Tokens (JWT).
    - [Creating Real-Time Applications](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/06-creating-real-time-applications.md): Real-time Node.js network applications, including collaborative tools, chat systems, live data dashboards, polling systems, Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementations, and WebSocket or socket.io integrations.
    - [Using Multiple Processes](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/07-using-multiple-processes.md): Node.js applications requiring vertical scaling, high-volume data parsing, CPU-heavy computations, multicore utilization, and the management or orchestration of multiple child processes or server clusters.
    - [Scaling Your Application](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/08-scaling-your-application.md): Scaling Node.js applications, implementing load balancing or reverse proxies (via http-proxy or NGINX), establishing inter-process or inter-server communication using message queues (RabbitMQ/AMQP) or UDP/multicasting, and integrating third-party cloud services including Amazon Web Services (S3, DynamoDB, SES) and Twilio.
    - [Microservices](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/09-microservices.md): Designing, refactoring, or building distributed architectures, specifically focusing on microservices, Service-Oriented Architectures (SOA), serverless deployments (AWS Lambda via Claudia.js), Seneca pattern-based microservices, Docker containerization, or Kubernetes container orchestration within a Node.js ecosystem.
    - [Testing Your Application](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/10-testing-your-application.md): Writing tests, debugging code, profiling Node.js applications, analyzing memory/CPU usage, or setting up/modifying test frameworks (including native assert, vm, Mocha, Chai, Sinon, Nightmare, or Puppeteer).
    - [Organizing Your Work into Modules](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/11-organizing-your-work-into-modules.md): Node.js module creation, file resolution, package management, dependency configuration, npm script automation, and package publishing workflows.
    - [Creating Your Own C++ Add-ons](.roo/vendor/node/mastering-node-js-second-edition-sandro-pasquali/12-creating-your-own-c-add-ons.md): Development, debugging, or optimization of native C++ add-ons for Node.js.
